volumeTitle: Children of Heracles. Hippolytus. Andromache. Hecuba
label: Hecuba (Section 555 - Section 720)

sections.label: Section 555
sections.original: οἳ δʼ , ὡς τάχιστʼ ἤκουσαν ὑστάτην ὄπα ,
sections.translation: So they set her free , as soon as they heard this last command from him whose might was over all . And she , hearing her master ’ s words , took her robe and tore it open from the shoulder to the waist ,

sections.label: Section 560
sections.original: μαστούς τʼ ἔδειξε στέρνα θʼ ὡς ἀγάλματος
sections.translation: displaying a breast and bosom fair as a statue ’ s ; then sinking on her knee , one word she spoke more piteous than all the rest , Young prince , if it is my breast you are eager to strike , see , here it is , strike home ! or if at my neck your sword

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: χρῄζεις , πάρεστι λαιμὸς εὐτρεπὴς ὅδε .
sections.translation: you will aim , that throat is here and ready .

sections.label: Section 566
sections.original: ὃ δʼ οὐ θέλων τε καὶ θέλων οἴκτῳ κόρης ,
sections.translation: Then he , half glad , half sorry in his pity for the maid , cut with the steel the channels of her breath , and streams of blood gushed forth ; but she , even in death , took good heed to fall with grace ,

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original: κρύπτουσʼ ἃ κρύπτειν ὄμματʼ ἀρσένων χρεών .
sections.translation: hiding from the gaze of men what must be hidden . When she had breathed her last through the fatal gash , no Argive set his hand to the same task , but some were strewing leaves over the corpse in handfuls , others bringing pine-logs

sections.label: Section 575
sections.original: κορμοὺς φέροντες πευκίνους , ὁ δʼ οὐ φέρων
sections.translation: and heaping up a pyre ; and the one who brought nothing would hear from him who did such taunts as these , Do you stand still , ignoble wretch , with no robe or ornament to bring for the maiden ? will you give nothing to her that showed such peerless bravery

sections.label: Section 580
sections.original: ψυχήν τʼ ἀρίστῃ ; τοιάδʼ ἀμφὶ σῆς λέγων
sections.translation: and spirit ? Such is the tale I tell about your daughter ’ s death , and regard you as blessed beyond all mothers in your noble child , yet crossed in fortune more than all .

sections.label: Section 583
sections.original: δεινόν τι πῆμα Πριαμίδαις ἐπέζεσεν
sections.translation: Upon the race of Priam and my city some fearful curse has burst ; it is sent by God , and we must bear it .

sections.label: Section 585
sections.original: ὦ θύγατερ , οὐκ οἶδʼ εἰς ὅ τι βλέψω κακῶν ,
sections.translation: O my daughter ! among this crowd of sorrows I do not know where to turn my gaze ; for if I set myself to one , another will not let me be ; while from this again another grief summons me , finding a successor to sorrow ’ s throne . And now I can not efface from my mind the memory of your sufferings

sections.label: Section 590
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν δυναίμην ἐξαλείψασθαι φρενός ·
sections.translation: sufficiently to stay my tears ; yet the story of your noble death has taken from the keenness of my grief . Is it not then strange that a poor land , when blessed by heaven with a lucky year , yields a good crop , while that which is good , if robbed of needful care ,

sections.label: Section 595
sections.original: κακὸν δίδωσι καρπόν , ἀνθρώποις δʼ ἀεὶ
sections.translation: bears little fruit ; yet among men the base is nothing else but wicked , the good man is good , never changing for the worse because of misfortune , but ever the same ? Is then the difference due to birth or bringing up ?

sections.label: Section 600
sections.original: ἔχει γε μέντοι καὶ τὸ θρεφθῆναι καλῶς
sections.translation: Good training doubtless gives lessons in good conduct , and if a man has mastered this , he knows what is shameful by the standard of the good . And these are random shafts from my mind , I know .

sections.label: Section 604
sections.original: σὺ δʼ ἐλθὲ καὶ σήμηνον Ἀργείοις τάδε ,
sections.translation: To Talthybius Go and proclaim to the Argives

sections.label: Section 605
sections.original: μὴ θιγγάνειν μοι μηδένʼ , ἀλλʼ εἴργειν ὄχλον ,
sections.translation: that they do not touch my daughter ’ s body but keep the crowd away . For when a countless army is gathered , the mob knows no restraint , and the unruliness of sailors exceeds that of fire , all abstinence from crime being counted criminal . Talthybius goes out .

sections.label: Section 609
sections.original: σὺ δʼ αὖ λαβοῦσα τεῦχος , ἀρχαία λάτρι ,
sections.translation: Addressing a servant Now you , my aged handmaid , take a pitcher

sections.label: Section 610
sections.original: βάψασʼ ἔνεγκε δεῦρο ποντίας ἁλός ,
sections.translation: and dip it in the salt sea and bring it here , that I for the last time may wash my child , an unwed bride , a ravished virgin , and lay her out , as she deserves , ah ! how can I ? impossible ! but as best I can ; and what will that amount to ?

sections.label: Section 615
sections.original: κόσμον τʼ ἀγείρασʼ αἰχμαλωτίδων πάρα ,
sections.translation: I will collect adornment from the captives , my companions in these tents , if perhaps any of them escaping her new master ’ s eye has made some theft from her home . The servant departs . O towering halls , O home so happy once ,

sections.label: Section 620
sections.original: ὦ πλεῖστʼ ἔχων κάλλιστά τʼ , εὐτεκνώτατε
sections.translation: O Priam , rich in store of fairest wealth , most blessed of fathers , and I no less , the grey-haired mother of your race , how are we brought to nothing , stripped of our former pride ! And in spite of all we vaunt ourselves , one on the riches of his house ,

sections.label: Section 625
sections.original: ὃ δʼ ἐν πολίταις τίμιος κεκλημένος .
sections.translation: another because he has an honored name among his fellow-citizens ! But these things are nothing ; in vain are all our thoughtful schemes , in vain our boastful words . He is happiest who meets no sorrow day by day . Hecuba enters the tent .

sections.label: Section 629
sections.original: ἐμοὶ χρῆν συμφοράν ,
sections.translation: Woe and tribulation

sections.label: Section 630
sections.original: ἐμοὶ χρῆν πημονὰν γενέσθαι ,
sections.translation: were made my lot in life , when Alexander first cut his beams of pine in Ida ’ s woods , to sail across the heaving sea

sections.label: Section 635
sections.original: Ἑλένας ἐπὶ λέκτρα , τὰν
sections.translation: in quest of Helen ’ s bed , loveliest woman on whom the sun-god turns his golden eye .

sections.label: Section 639
sections.original: πόνοι γὰρ καὶ πόνων
sections.translation: For here begins trouble ’ s cycle ,

sections.label: Section 640
sections.original: ἀνάγκαι κρείσσονες κυκλοῦνται
sections.translation: and , worse than that , relentless fate ; and from one man ’ s folly came a universal curse , bringing death to the land of Simois , with trouble from an alien shore . The strife the shepherd decided

sections.label: Section 645
sections.original: δᾳ κρίνει τρισσὰς μακάρων
sections.translation: on Ida , between three daughters of the blessed gods ,

sections.label: Section 648
sections.original: ἐπὶ δορὶ καὶ φόνῳ καὶ ἐμῶν μελάθρων λώβᾳ ·
sections.translation: brought as its result war and bloodshed and the ruin of my home ;

sections.label: Section 650
sections.original: στένει δὲ καί τις ἀμφὶ τὸν εὔροον Εὐρώταν
sections.translation: and many a Spartan maiden too is weeping bitter tears in her halls on the banks of fair Eurotas , and many a mother whose sons are slain ,

sections.label: Section 655
sections.original: τίθεται χέρα δρύπτεται παρειάν ,
sections.translation: is smiting her gray head and tearing her cheeks , making her nails bloody in the furrowed gash .

sections.label: Section 658
sections.original: γυναῖκες , Ἑκάβη ποῦ ποθʼ ἡ παναθλία ,
sections.translation: Oh ! ladies , where is Hecuba , our queen of sorrow , who conquers all in tribulation , men and women both alike ?

sections.label: Section 660
sections.original: κακοῖσιν ; οὐδεὶς στέφανον ἀνθαιρήσεται .
sections.translation: No one shall dispute the crown with her .

sections.label: Section 661
sections.original: τί δʼ , ὦ τάλαινα σῆς κακογλώσσου βοῆς ;
sections.translation: What now , unhappy one with your cry of misery ? Your evil tidings never seem to rest .

sections.label: Section 663
sections.original: Ἑκάβῃ φέρω τόδʼ ἄλγος · ἐν κακοῖσι δὲ
sections.translation: It is to Hecuba I bring my bitter news ; no easy task is it for mortal lips to speak smooth words in sorrow .

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: καὶ μὴν περῶσα τυγχάνει δόμων ὕπερ
sections.translation: Look , she is coming even now from the shelter of the tent , appearing just in time to hear you speak . Hecuba comes out of the tent .

sections.label: Section 667
sections.original: ὦ παντάλαινα κἄτι μᾶλλον ἢ λέγω ,
sections.translation: O mistress , most hapless beyond all words of mine to tell ; you are ruined , you no longer exist , though you are alive ; of children , husband , city bereft ; hopelessly undone !

sections.label: Section 670
sections.original: οὐ καινὸν εἶπας , εἰδόσιν δʼ ὠνείδισας .
sections.translation: This is no news but insult ; I have heard it all before . But why have you come , bringing here to me the corpse of Polyxena , on whose burial Achaea ’ s army was reported to be busily engaged ?

sections.label: Section 674
sections.original: ἥδʼ οὐδὲν οἶδεν , ἀλλά μοι Πολυξένην
sections.translation: She knows nothing , but mourns

sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: θρηνεῖ , νέων δὲ πημάτων οὐχ ἅπτεται .
sections.translation: Polyxena , not grasping her new sorrows .

sections.label: Section 676
sections.original: οἲ ʼγὼ τάλαινα · μῶν τὸ βακχεῖον κάρα
sections.translation: Ah ! woe is me ! you are surely not bringing here frenzied Cassandra , the prophetic maid ?

sections.label: Section 678
sections.original: ζῶσαν λέλακας , τὸν θανόντα δʼ οὐ στένεις
sections.translation: You speak of the living ; but the dead you do not weep is here . Uncovering the corpse Mark well the body now laid bare ;

sections.label: Section 680
sections.original: εἴ σοι φανεῖται θαῦμα καὶ παρʼ ἐλπίδας .
sections.translation: is not this a sight to fill you with wonder , and upset your hopes ?

sections.label: Section 681
sections.original: οἴμοι , βλέπω δὴ παῖδʼ ἐμὸν τεθνηκότα ,
sections.translation: Ah me ! it is the corpse of my son Polydorus I behold , whom the Thracian man was keeping safe for me in his halls . Alas ! this is the end of all ; my life is over . chanting O my son , my son ,

sections.label: Section 685
sections.original: αἰαῖ , κατάρχομαι γόων ,
sections.translation: alas ! I now begin the laments , a frantic strain I learned just now from some avenging fiend .

sections.label: Section 688
sections.original: ἔγνως γὰρ ἄτην παιδός , ὦ δύστηνε σύ ;
sections.translation: What ! so you knew your son ’ s fate , poor lady ?

sections.label: Section 689
sections.original: ἄπιστʼ ἄπιστα , καινὰ καινὰ δέρκομαι .
sections.translation: I cannot , cannot credit this fresh sight I see .

sections.label: Section 690
sections.original: ἕτερα δʼ ἀφʼ ἑτέρων κακὰ κακῶν κυρεῖ ·
sections.translation: One woe succeeds to another ; no day will ever pass without groans and tears .

sections.label: Section 693
sections.original: δείνʼ , ὦ τάλαινα , δεινὰ πάσχομεν κακά .
sections.translation: Alas ! poor lady , our sufferings are cruel indeed .

sections.label: Section 694
sections.original: ὦ τέκνον τέκνον ταλαίνας ματρός ,
sections.translation: O my son , child of a luckless mother ,

sections.label: Section 695
sections.original: τίνι μόρῳ θνῄσκεις ,
sections.translation: what was the manner of your death ? by what fate do you lie here ? by whose hands ?

sections.label: Section 698
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδʼ · ἐπʼ ἀκταῖς νιν κυρῶ θαλασσίαις ⋯
sections.translation: I do not know . I found him on the sea-shore .

sections.label: Section 699
sections.original: ἔκβλητον , ἢ πέσημα φοινίου δορός ,
sections.translation: Cast up on the smooth sand , or thrown there

sections.label: Section 700
sections.original: ἐν ψαμάθῳ λευρᾷ ;
sections.translation: after the murderous blow ?

sections.label: Section 701
sections.original: πόντου νιν ἐξήνεγκε πελάγιος κλύδων .
sections.translation: The waves had washed him ashore .

sections.label: Section 702
sections.original: ὤμοι , αἰαῖ , ἔμαθον ἔνυπνον ὀμμάτων
sections.translation: Alas ! alas ! I now know the vision I saw in my sleep ; the dusky-winged phantom

sections.label: Section 705
sections.original: φάσμα μελανόπτερον , τὰν ἐσεῖδον ἀμφὶ σέ ,
sections.translation: did not escape me , the vision I saw of you , my son , now no more within the bright sunshine .

sections.label: Section 709
sections.original: τίς γάρ νιν ἔκτεινʼ ; οἶσθʼ ὀνειρόφρων φράσαι ;
sections.translation: Who slew him then ? Can your dream-lore tell us that ?

sections.label: Section 710
sections.original: ἐμὸς ἐμὸς ξένος , Θρῄκιος ἱππότας ,
sections.translation: It was my own familiar friend , the knight of Thrace , with whom his aged father had placed the boy in hiding .

sections.label: Section 713
sections.original: οἴμοι , τί λέξεις ; χρυσὸν ὡς ἔχοι κτανών ;
sections.translation: O horror ! what will you say ? did he slay him to get the gold ?

sections.label: Section 714
sections.original: ἄρρητʼ ἀνωνόμαστα , θαυμάτων πέρα ,
sections.translation: O dreadful crime ! O deed without a name ! beyond wonder !

sections.label: Section 715
sections.original: οὐχ ὅσιʼ οὐδʼ ἀνεκτά . ποῦ δίκα ξένων ;
sections.translation: impious ! intolerable ! Where are the laws between guest and host ? Accursed of men ! how have you mangled his flesh , slashing the poor child ’ s limbs

sections.label: Section 720
sections.original: μέλεα τοῦδε παιδὸς οὐδʼ ᾤκτισας .
sections.translation: with ruthless sword , lost to all sense of pity !
